feuds:
Kyle Richards: Camille calls Kyle and Teddi's friendship 'creepy' and implies Teddi is 'up Kyle's ass,' escalating tension on the reunion stage
Teddi Mellencamp Arroyave: Camille criticizes Teddi for cutting off Kyle during conversations and questions the authenticity of their bridesmaid relationship
Denise Richards: Camille makes pointed remarks about Denise not having a prenup with Charlie Sheen and implies Denise sat back 'buying fancy clothes and shoving bonbons' in her face instead of working
key_moments:
Camille tells the host she found Kyle and Teddi's friendship 'creepy,' saying 'Teddy was like up Kyle's eyes' and that Teddi cuts her off whenever she tries to speak to Kyle
Camille takes a sharp jab at Denise's divorce settlement: 'I worked hard I just didn't sit back buying fancy clothes and shoving bonbons in my face'
Camille questions the sincerity of Kyle asking Teddi to be a bridesmaid: 'asking her to be your bridesmaid when you don't hang out outside of the show'
Camille is called out on-stage with the line 'you agreed that their friendship is quote creepy — why do you say that?', forcing her to defend her comments
After Camille's prenup remarks about Denise, other cast members react off-camera: 'I think she's just being really mean'
talking_points:
Stand firm on the Teddi/Kyle friendship observation — you've known Kyle for over a decade and feel genuinely shut out by a newer cast member
Be prepared to justify the prenup/Charlie Sheen comments about Denise — frame it as speaking from personal experience, not attacking her
Address why you were invited as a bridesmaid to Kyle's wedding despite limited off-camera contact this year
Own your 'creepy' tweet comment — don't backpedal, but clarify what you meant by it on your own terms
Acknowledge the audience and host pushback gracefully — you don't want to come across as the villain when you're a 'Friend of' trying to carve out relevance
confessional_draft: I've known Kyle Richards for ten years — TEN years — and suddenly I can't get a word in edgewise because Teddi is always right there, cutting me off. That's not a friendship, that's a shadow. And as for Denise? I lived through one of the most public divorces in Hollywood history, so if I have thoughts about prenups, I think I've earned the right to say them.

feuds:
Camille Grammer: Camille publicly accuses their friendship dynamic with Teddi of being 'creepy' and implies Kyle replaced Lisa Vanderpump with Teddi the moment LVP stepped out
Lisa Vanderpump: Referenced indirectly — host notes LVP is absent from the reunion entirely, and Camille implies Kyle positioned herself strategically once LVP was gone
key_moments:
Kyle is put on the spot when the host reads Camille's tweet calling her friendship with Teddi 'creepy'
Camille says to Kyle directly: 'you made sure that as soon as Lisa Vanderpump was out that you were right there' — a pointed accusation of opportunism
Kyle defends her long-standing friendship with Teddi: 'I've known her for a very long time'
Kyle seems visibly caught off guard and deflects Camille's 'bridesmaid' accusation by insisting she and Teddi do have a genuine bond
talking_points:
Defend your friendship with Teddi — it's genuine, it predates this season, and you won't let Camille reframe it as something calculated
Address the LVP absence head-on — where does your friendship with her stand now that she's not at the reunion?
Don't let Camille's 'bridesmaid' jab go unanswered — clarify the timeline and depth of your relationship with Teddi
Stay composed; getting flustered plays into Camille's narrative that she's hitting a nerve
confessional_draft: Camille has known me for ten years, and she's going to sit there and call my friendship with Teddi 'creepy'? I don't need to explain who my real friends are to someone who only shows up when the cameras are rolling. But sure, Camille — let's talk about who's really inserting themselves where they don't belong.

feuds: Camille Grammer: Camille singles out Teddi for allegedly cutting off conversations between Camille and Kyle, and questions the authenticity of Teddi's bridesmaid role at Kyle's wedding
key_moments:
Teddi is called out by Camille on-stage: 'Teddy's there to cut you off and that's annoying'
In a meta moment during the taping, Teddi interrupts to prove Camille's very point: 'I have a question — I'm cutting you off okay, but... there's my point'
Teddi's closeness with Kyle is described as potentially 'codependent' by an audience tweet, which the host reads aloud
Camille implies Teddi only grew close to Kyle as a strategic move once LVP was sidelined
talking_points:
Push back on Camille's 'up Kyle's ass' characterization — your friendship with Kyle is mutual and organic
Address the codependency tweet directly and with humor — you're confident enough in your friendship not to be rattled
Clarify that your closeness with Kyle is not about LVP's absence — it developed naturally during Season 9
Don't let the bridesmaid comment slide — affirm your genuine long-standing connection with Kyle
confessional_draft: Camille called our friendship creepy, and then the host literally watched me cut someone off mid-sentence and said 'there's my point.' I can't win! But here's the truth — Kyle and I are close because we actually like each other, and no tweet or reunion hot take is going to change that.

feuds: Camille Grammer: Camille takes unprompted jabs at Denise's divorce from Charlie Sheen, implying she didn't work hard and made poor financial decisions by not pursuing a larger settlement
key_moments:
Denise reveals she did not have a prenup with Charlie Sheen and chose not to take half his earnings upon divorce: 'I could have asked for half of what he made and I did not'
Camille fires back with: 'I worked hard I just didn't sit back buying fancy clothes and shoving bonbons in my face' — a thinly veiled dig at Denise
Denise briefly addresses Aaron calling Charlie ahead of their wedding out of respect: 'Aaron called Charlie to let him know that we were getting married... just out of respect that he was gonna be a stepdad to our girls'
Denise opens up emotionally about her daughter Eloise's chromosome disorder (deletion on chromosome 8) and the difficulty of receiving the diagnosis: 'it was hard to see it in black and white'
Denise is heard saying 'stop it, it is not true' in the cold open — suggesting she defends herself against an accusation during the reunion
talking_points:
Stand behind your decision not to pursue a larger settlement from Charlie Sheen — it was your choice and reflects your values, not naivety
Be prepared to address Camille's 'bonbons' comment directly — don't let it go unchallenged
Share openly about Eloise's diagnosis — it's a deeply personal moment that humanizes you to the audience
Highlight your co-parenting relationship with Charlie and Aaron's respectful introduction — it shows maturity and class
Own your 'very direct demeanor' — it's your brand and your strength this season
confessional_draft: I walked away from millions because that was the right thing to do for my family — that's called integrity, Camille, not sitting around eating bonbons. And while everyone's busy dissecting my divorce, I'm focused on my daughter, my husband, and the life we've built together. That's what actually matters.

feuds: Kyle Richards / Group: Her complete absence from the reunion is a storyline unto itself — her non-attendance is referenced multiple times as a source of tension and unresolved conflict
key_moments:
Host confirms LVP is not present at the reunion at all: 'Lisa Vanderpump's not here — has she not been there at all for this reunion?'
Camille implies Kyle filled the void left by LVP's exit strategically, suggesting the group dynamic shifted around her absence
Cold open teases 'trouble afoot in Beverly Hills' — framed in part around LVP's absence and the fallout from the season
talking_points:
Her absence is itself the statement — the reunion cannot avoid discussing why she chose not to attend
The Kyle/LVP friendship fracture is unresolved and looms over every segment
Camille's comments about Kyle 'moving in' once LVP was gone speak to the perceived power vacuum her exit created
Her non-attendance may be framed as a de-escalation or as avoidance, depending on the edit
confessional_draft: I've given this franchise everything — my restaurants, my home, my grief. And when I needed my friends to show up for me, they chose a different path. So no, I won't be sitting on that stage. Some things are simply not worth the price of admission.

feuds: Unspecified castmate: The cold open references 'why does Erika not want to be around you' directed at someone on stage, suggesting a simmering social tension
key_moments:
Cold open includes the line 'why do you think that Erica doesn't want to be around you' — suggesting Erika's social withdrawal from a cast member is addressed at the reunion
Referenced in the cold open's broader teaser of drama: 'there's major things happening, there's trouble afoot'
talking_points:
Address the 'doesn't want to be around you' comment — clarify who it was directed at and your reasoning
Speak to the emotional and financial stakes of the Erika Jayne National Tour this season
Maintain your composed, unbothered persona — let the drama come to you, don't chase it
confessional_draft: I don't dislike anyone. I simply choose where I spend my energy very carefully. When you're running a national tour and building an empire, you can't afford to waste time on people who don't bring something real to the table. That's not cold — that's called standards.

feuds: General group tension: Rinna is referenced in the cold open montage amid broader cast conflict and the 'honesty' theme of the season
key_moments:
Cold open features Rinna in the group montage with the line 'I paid the ultimate price by being honest' — suggesting she owns a controversial truth-telling moment from the season
Referenced in the broader reunion teaser: 'we've all said things privately about each other'
talking_points:
Own the 'I paid the ultimate price by being honest' moment — define what that price was and whether you'd do it again
Address the season's overarching honesty vs. loyalty debate and where you land
Don't shy away from the group's fractures — your role as a truth-teller is central to your brand
confessional_draft: I've always said the truth is the truth, and this season proved it costs you. But would I take it back? Not for a second. If telling the truth makes me the villain, then fine — I'll own that tiara too.

feuds: Unspecified: Cold open references 'my husband did not scam anyone and I don't appreciate that' — suggesting Dorit defends PK against financial allegations during the reunion
key_moments:
Dorit is heard in the cold open declaring: 'my husband did not scam anyone and I don't appreciate that' — a pointed defense of PK amid apparent accusations from the group
Referenced in the broader reunion montage of confrontations and emotional moments
talking_points:
Come prepared to defend PK against any financial misconduct allegations raised this season
Address your swimwear brand expansion and the pressures that came with it — show growth and accountability
Don't get defensive about the PK situation in a way that reads as dismissive — own the conversation
confessional_draft: My husband has worked incredibly hard to build what we have, and I will not sit here and let anyone paint him as something he is not. You want to come for my family? Then you better come with facts, not rumors. Because I will shut that down every single time.
